provider_description: A news aggregator that respects your attention. No clickbait. No infinite scroll. News sessions with beginnings and endings.

identity.ethos: Informed citizens, not addicted users. Transparency over engagement. Signal over noise.
identity.content_policy: No sensationalism. No manipulation. Headlines reflect content accurately.

suggested_shell.description: News consumed in focused reading sessions, not infinite feeds

core_features.description: Top headlines organized by category with summaries
core_features.guidance: Show 8-12 headlines with 1-2 sentence summaries. Group by category. Most important stories first within each category. No ranking by engagement metrics.

core_features.description: Focused article reading with session progress
core_features.guidance: When user clicks a headline, show full article in a clean reading view. Include session progress indicator (e.g., 3 of 10 articles read). Provide 'next article' suggestion based on category balance, not clickbait.

core_features.description: User controls which categories appear and their weight
core_features.guidance: Simple toggles or sliders for each category. Changes take effect immediately. No dark patterns to re-enable disabled categories.
When session ends (time or article limit), show brief summary of what was read. No guilt-tripping to continue. Clean exit.

design_principles: No infinite scroll — session has a defined end
No clickbait headlines — summaries are accurate
No engagement metrics visible (no view counts, no trending badges)
Reading time estimates on every article
Clean typography optimized for reading, not scanning
Dark mode support based on system preference
forbidden_patterns: Breaking news alerts or urgency manipulation
Social sharing prompts that interrupt reading

data_contracts.consumer_data_policy: No data leaves the consumer's device. Reading history stored locally only.
data_contracts.source_transparency: All articles include original source attribution and publication date.
data_contracts.enrichment_transparency: Category assignments and importance rankings are visible to the consumer.

sample_data.note: In production, this would be a live SEP endpoint. For this demo, the provider agent generates representative content.
